attrs.alt: Lifecycle Workflows: joiner, mover, and leaver automation with available built-in tasks and trigger conditions

content.text: Lifecycle Workflows is an Entra ID Governance feature (generally available 2023) that automates identity provisioning tasks triggered by user attribute changes or time-based events. The three workflow categories — joiner, mover, and leaver — map to the identity lifecycle events that previously required IT tickets or manual scripting: onboarding a new hire, changing a user's access when they transfer departments, and revoking everything when they leave.

Joiner workflows fire before or on the employee hire date (employeeHireDate attribute). Tasks include generating a Temporary Access Pass, adding the user to onboarding groups, requesting access packages, enabling the account, and calling custom Logic Apps to integrate with badge or HR systems.

Mover workflows fire when a user attribute changes — typically department, jobTitle, or companyName. Tasks switch group memberships and access packages to reflect the new role without requiring a ticket.

Leaver workflows fire on or after the employee leave date (employeeLeaveDateTime attribute). Tasks disable the account, revoke active sign-in sessions, remove group memberships, revoke access packages, and eventually soft-delete the account after a retention period.

Lifecycle Workflows do not replace HR-driven provisioning (e.g., Workday or SAP SuccessFactors via Entra cloud sync). They complement it: the HR system writes employeeHireDate and employeeLeaveDateTime to Entra, and Lifecycle Workflows react to those attributes.
Creates a time-limited TAP for passwordless first sign-in; emails it to the user or manager
Sets accountEnabled=true — un-blocks sign-in on hire date
Adds user to specified security or M365 groups
Submits an adminAdd request for a specified access package and policy
Sends a custom email to the user, their manager, or a specified mailbox
Submits an adminRemove request for all or specific access package assignments
Removes the user from every group they are a member of
Removes the user from all Teams channels and groups
Invalidates all refresh tokens and active sessions (equivalent to Revoke-MgUserSignInSession)
Soft-deletes the user account (30-day recovery window applies)
Calls an Azure Logic App via a custom extension callback URL — for integrations outside Entra

A workflow definition specifies the category (joiner/mover/leaver), trigger conditions, an optional user scope filter (which users this workflow applies to), and the ordered list of tasks. Workflows are evaluated by the engine every 3 hours.

Leaver workflows should be configured conservatively — the order of tasks matters. Revoking sessions and disabling the account should happen before removing group memberships, to avoid a window where the account is removed from monitoring groups but still has active tokens.

The triggerCustomTaskExtension task calls an Azure Logic App via an HTTP callback. This is how you integrate with systems outside Entra: creating a badge request in a physical access system, opening an ITSM ticket, sending a Teams message with onboarding checklist, or updating a headcount database.

The Logic App receives a payload from Lifecycle Workflows containing the user's objectId and display name, plus the workflow and task IDs. The Logic App can then call back to Graph to read any other user attributes it needs.

Each workflow run for each user is recorded in the execution history. Task-level results show which tasks succeeded, failed, or were skipped. This is the primary debugging tool when a lifecycle event does not produce the expected outcome.

# List all workflows
$workflows = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows'
$workflows.value | Select-Object id, displayName, category, isEnabled

# Get execution history for a specific workflow
$wfId = '<workflow-id>'
$runs = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows/$wfId/runs"

$runs.value | Select-Object id, startedDateTime, completedDateTime, totalTasksCount,
    failedTasksCount, @{N='Status';E={$_.status}}

# Drill into a specific run to see per-user, per-task results
$runId = $runs.value[0].id
$userProcessResults = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows/$wfId/runs/$runId/userProcessingResults?`$expand=taskProcessingResults"

foreach ($upr in $userProcessResults.value) {
    Write-Host "User: $($upr.subject.displayName)  Status: $($upr.processingStatus)"
    foreach ($t in $upr.taskProcessingResults) {
        Write-Host "  Task: $($t.task.displayName)  Result: $($t.processingStatus)"
        if ($t.failureReason) { Write-Host "    Error: $($t.failureReason)" }
    }
}

Lifecycle Workflows can be triggered on-demand for a specific user without waiting for the next scheduled evaluation. This is useful for testing new workflows, re-running a failed workflow after fixing an error, or accelerating an emergency off-boarding.

# Trigger a leaver workflow on-demand for a specific user
$activateBody = @{
    subjects = @(
        @{ id = '<user-object-id>' }
    )
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows/<workflow-id>/activate" \
  -Body $activateBody -ContentType 'application/json'

# The activation creates an on-demand run; poll userProcessingResults to check completion
Start-Sleep -Seconds 30
$results = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows/<workflow-id>/userProcessingResults?`$filter=subject/id eq '<user-object-id>'"
Write-Host "Processing status: $($results.value[0].processingStatus)"
Basic joiner/leaver workflows with built-in tasks

Lifecycle Workflows automate the three identity lifecycle events — joiner (new hire), mover (role change), and leaver (departure) — using time-based or attribute-change triggers against user profiles in Entra ID.

Built-in tasks cover the most common operations: generate Temporary Access Pass, enable/disable account, revoke sessions, add/remove group memberships, grant/revoke access packages, and delete accounts.
Custom task extensions call Azure Logic Apps, enabling integration with any external system — badge access, ITSM, HR, equipment management — without custom code running inside Entra.
Task order matters for leaver workflows: always disable and revoke sessions before removing group memberships to avoid a window with active tokens but no monitoring group membership.
Execution history is stored 30 days per run, with per-user and per-task result granularity. Use on-demand activation for testing and emergency off-boarding scenarios.
Mover workflows and custom task extensions require the Entra ID Governance license; basic joiner/leaver built-in tasks are available with P2.
